methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rangeview is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Cameron Schiller and Aeden Gasser-Brennan. The company has raised $5M+ to date. The relevant comp universe comprises nine private robotics and manufacturing firms (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and one public comparable.

Stage-progression valuation was the only applicable triangulation method. Using $5M in total capital raised as a proxy for seed-stage valuation and applying a standard 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next financing stage yielded an implied valuation of $12M. Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing sector—while seven same-sector peers exist, none met the threshold of three minimum datapoints when filtered by stage and valuation tier.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median point of $12M. This estimate carries material uncertainty given the single-method basis and the absence of reliable peer transaction data at seed stage in this sector. Key sensitivities include the magnitude of the stage-progression multiplier and the company's actual progress toward next-round milestones. Additional fundraising velocity or technical de-risking could support movement toward the higher end of the range.
